etymology.description: 聿(聿) (yù, "writing brush") [phonetic] originally depicting a brush -> 竹(竹) (zhú, "bamboo") added [semantic] to indicate material used -> writing brush
etymology.interpretation: The character 筆(bǐ, "writing brush") is a phono-semantic compound where 聿(聿) (yù, "writing brush") provides the pronunciation as it originally depicted a brush. 竹(竹) (zhú, "bamboo") was added to specify the semantic field by indicating that the traditional writing brush was made of bamboo.
etymology.simplification: 笔(筆): The hair component 毛(毛) (máo, "hair") replaced 聿(聿) (yù) to visually emphasize the traditional materials used in the construction of the brush, simplifying the form.

examples.chinese: 我买了一支新笔(我買了一支新筆)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ mǎile yī zhī xīn bǐ.
examples.english: I bought a new writing brush.

examples.chinese: 他用毛笔写字(他用毛筆寫字)。
examples.english: He writes with a writing brush.

examples.chinese: 笔的历史悠久(筆的歷史悠久)。
examples.english: The writing brush has a long history.
